src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog package

Submodules

src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog.MonitorCase module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog.MonitorCase.MonitorCase(case_path, modules, reprocess_case)[source]

Bases: object

Monitors a specified case path for changes that trigger module actions based on the defined module configurations.

__init__(case_path, modules, reprocess_case)[source]

Initializes the monitoring setup with the specified case path and modules.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – The path to the directory to be monitored.

  • modules (list) – List of modules to apply to the monitoring events.

  • reprocess_case (bool) – If True, it will reprocess all the files. If False, files that were present during previous execution will not be processed.

on_inactivity()[source]

Method to be called when inactivity is detected.

setup_handler()[source]

Sets up file and directory event handlers for each module, configuring and starting an observer to monitor the filesystem.

start()[source]

src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog.WatchdogService module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog.WatchdogService.CaseSnapshot(case_path)[source]

Bases: object

Class to snapshot the directory entries.

__init__(case_path)[source]

Initialize the CaseSnapshot with the given case path.

Parameters:

case_path (str) – The path of the case to snapshot.

get_entries_set()[source]

Get a set of the directory entries for easy comparison.

Returns:

A set of tuples representing the entries.

Return type:

set

scan_directory()[source]

Scan the directory and subdirectories to record entries.

class src.osir_service.osir_service.watchdog.WatchdogService.ModuleHandler(case_path: Path, cooldown_period: int, module_instances: list[OsirModuleModel], case_uuid, handler_uuid=None)[source]

Bases: FileSystemEventHandler

Handles file system events to trigger processing modules based on file or directory changes that match specified patterns.

__init__(case_path: Path, cooldown_period: int, module_instances: list[OsirModuleModel], case_uuid, handler_uuid=None)[source]

Initializes the ModuleHandler with configurations to monitor file and directory events related to a specific case.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – Base path on master’s disk of the case to monitor.

  • modules_info (list) – List of module information dicts.

  • cooldown_period (int) – Cooldown period between checks in seconds.

  • module_instances (list) – List of module instances to execute.

  • case_uuid (str) – Unique identifier for the case.

check_match(src_path, pattern, module_name)[source]
handle_directory_event(event, module_instance: OsirModuleModel)[source]

Processes directory events that match configured patterns and sets up checks for idleness.

Parameters:

  • event – The event object representing the directory event.

  • module_instance (OsirModuleModel) – Module configuration instance

monitor_directory(case_path, interval=5, reprocess=False)[source]

Monitors the directory for changes at specified intervals.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – Path of the case to monitor.

  • interval (int, optional) – Interval in seconds to wait between scans. Default is 5 seconds.

  • reprocess (bool) – If True, it will reprocess all the files. If False, files that were present during previous execution will not be processed.

on_created(event)[source]

Specific handler for ‘created’ events, which are triggered when a new file or directory is created.

Parameters:

event – The event object representing the file or directory creation event.

on_created_new(event, module: OsirModuleModel)[source]

Handles the creation of new files or directories by checking them against module-specific filtering rules before triggering processing.

Parameters:

  • event (watchdog.events.FileSystemEvent) – The event object representing the file system change.

  • module (OsirModuleModel) – The module configuration containing input types, patterns, and processing logic.

Returns:

This method performs actions (logging, triggering processing)

but does not return a value.

Return type:

None

process(file_match: Path, module_instance: OsirModuleModel)[source]

Initiates processing of a file or directory based on the module configuration.

Parameters:

  • match_path (Path) – Path to the file or directory that matched the module input rules.

  • module_instance (OsirModuleModel) – Module configuration instance to execute (will be deep-copied and updated).
